project.name: CareerPilot
project.description: CareerPilot_IUT_Shonghorsho: Codesprint 2026. A job application automation platform.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Specifies files and directories to be excluded from the Docker build context to optimize image size and security.
nodes.languageNotes: en

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Environment configuration template providing placeholders for database connections, LLM API keys, browser automation settings, and platform credentials.
nodes.languageNotes: en

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Core project guide containing architecture details, directory structure, coding standards, and common development commands.
nodes.languageNotes: en

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Main project entry documentation, currently serving as a placeholder with the project title.
nodes.languageNotes: en

nodes.name: docker-compose.dev.yml
nodes.summary: Development-specific Docker Compose override that enables hot reloading for backend and worker services via volume mounting.
nodes.languageNotes: en

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Primary Docker Compose configuration defining the application's multi-container architecture, including backend, frontend, worker, and Redis services.
nodes.languageNotes: en

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Project-level Python configuration specifying dependencies for core logic, AI/LLM integration, browser automation, and development tools.
nodes.languageNotes: en

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Root HTML entry point for the AutoApply AI React application, defining the DOM mount point and loading the primary TypeScript entry module.
nodes.languageNotes: HTML5 document using ES modules for script loading.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend manifest managing core dependencies (React 18, MUI 5, TanStack Query, Zustand), build automation scripts, and testing infrastructure.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON manifest for dependency and script management.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Main TypeScript configuration for the frontend source code, enabling strict type checking, React JSX support, and modern module resolution.
nodes.languageNotes: Compiler options for source code type checking and transpilation targeting modern browsers.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: Specialized TypeScript configuration for build-time Node.js environments and Vite configuration files.
nodes.languageNotes: Compiler options for build-time configuration files.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Project-specific ignore patterns for the Knowledge Graph analysis, excluding noise files like node_modules and logs.
nodes.languageNotes: Custom ignore format based on .gitignore syntax.

nodes.name: alembic.ini
nodes.summary: Alembic configuration file for managing database migrations, specifying connection settings and logging.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard INI configuration format.

nodes.name: deps.py
nodes.summary: API dependencies for FastAPI, providing database session and Redis client injection.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python generators for resource management (yield).

nodes.name: get_db
nodes.summary: Dependency that yields a database session and ensures it's closed after the request.
nodes.languageNotes: Yields Session from SQLAlchemy.

nodes.name: get_redis
nodes.summary: Dependency that yields a Redis connection for caching or message queuing.
nodes.languageNotes: Yields Redis client.

nodes.name: analytics.py
nodes.summary: API router for analytics endpoints, providing data for dashboards, funnels, and usage metrics.
nodes.languageNotes: FastAPI APIRouter with dependency injection.

nodes.name: GET /analytics/dashboard
nodes.summary: Returns high-level statistics for the main dashboard.
nodes.languageNotes: Returns DashboardStats schema.

nodes.name: applications.py
nodes.summary: API router for managing job applications, including creation, listing, and status updates.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements CRUD operations for Application models.

nodes.name: jobs.py
nodes.summary: API router for job management, featuring job search, listing, and ATS analysis integration.
nodes.languageNotes: Integrates job_service and ATS scoring.

nodes.name: resumes.py
nodes.summary: API router for resume operations: upload, parsing, scoring, optimization, and profile extraction.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes complex heuristic-based profile extraction logic.

nodes.name: extract_profile_from_resume
nodes.summary: Heuristic-based extraction of candidate details (contact, skills, experience) from resume text.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses regex and section markers to identify profile components.

nodes.name: router.py
nodes.summary: Main API v1 router that aggregates all sub-routers (analytics, applications, jobs, resumes, settings).
nodes.languageNotes: Central hub for V1 API routes.

nodes.name: settings.py
nodes.summary: API router for user and application settings, including LLM provider management.
nodes.languageNotes: Manages UserSettings model persistence.

nodes.name: WebSocket /ws
nodes.summary: Main WebSocket endpoint for real-time updates and notifications.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses FastAPI WebSocket support with ConnectionManager.

nodes.name: ConnectionManager
nodes.summary: Manages active WebSocket connections, handling broadcasts and targeted messages.
nodes.languageNotes: Thread-safe list of active connections.

nodes.name: ApplicationStatus
nodes.summary: Enumeration of possible states for a job application (Draft, Applied, Interview, etc.).
nodes.languageNotes: Inherits from Python Enum.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Main configuration class for the backend, loading environment variables for DB, Redis, and external APIs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Pydantic BaseSettings for validation.

nodes.name: ExperienceAnalyzer
nodes.summary: Analyzes candidate experience against job descriptions, calculating seniority alignment and responsibility matching.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses weighted heuristics for seniority scoring.

nodes.name: KeywordAnalyzer
nodes.summary: Performs keyword extraction and overlap analysis between resumes and job descriptions using TF-IDF and semantic similarity.
nodes.languageNotes: Integrates SpaCy for NLP tasks.

nodes.name: ATSOptimizer
nodes.summary: Suggests resume improvements based on ATS analysis gaps and industry-standard keywords.
nodes.languageNotes: Generates natural language suggestions for candidates.

nodes.name: ResumeScorer
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full ATS scoring process, combining skill, experience, and keyword analysis into a final score.
nodes.languageNotes: Aggregates scores from multiple specialized analyzers.

nodes.name: SkillMatcher
nodes.summary: Handles skill normalization and fuzzy matching between candidate skills and job requirements.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SequenceMatcher for string similarity.

nodes.name: automation/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Initialization file for the automation core module.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard Python package initializer.

nodes.name: agent.py
nodes.summary: Defines the BrowserAgent which wraps the browser-use package for AI-driven browser navigation and task execution.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pydantic for model configuration and structlog for logging.

nodes.name: BrowserAgent
nodes.summary: A high-level agent that manages browser sessions and executes natural language tasks using an LLM.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements async context manager interface (__aenter__, __aexit__).

nodes.name: platforms/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Initialization for job platform integrations.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package initializer for platforms.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Base classes and data models for job platform integrations, defining the standard interface for all job boards.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ABC for abstract base classes and Pydantic for data models.

nodes.name: JobListing
nodes.summary: A normalized data model representing a job listing from any platform.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen pydantic model for immutability.

nodes.name: JobPlatform
nodes.summary: Abstract base class defining the required interface for job platform integrations (login, search, apply, etc.).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @abstractmethod to enforce implementation in subclasses.

nodes.name: glassdoor.py
nodes.summary: Glassdoor platform integration implementing login, job search, and application submission using BrowserAgent.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains complex parsing logic for search results and job details.

nodes.name: GlassdoorPlatform
nodes.summary: Concrete implementation of JobPlatform for Glassdoor.
nodes.languageNotes: Inherits from JobPlatform.

nodes.name: indeed.py
nodes.summary: Indeed platform integration implementing login, job search, and application submission using BrowserAgent.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes platform-specific URL handling and result parsing.

nodes.name: IndeedPlatform
nodes.summary: Concrete implementation of JobPlatform for Indeed.
nodes.languageNotes: Inherits from JobPlatform.

nodes.name: linkedin.py
nodes.summary: LinkedIn platform integration implementing login, job search, and application submission via 'Easy Apply' using BrowserAgent.
nodes.languageNotes: Focuses on 'Easy Apply' workflow automation.

nodes.name: LinkedInPlatform
nodes.summary: Concrete implementation of JobPlatform for LinkedIn.
nodes.languageNotes: Inherits from JobPlatform.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Registry for managing and dynamically instantiating job platform plugins.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the Registry pattern for platform discovery.

nodes.name: PlatformRegistry
nodes.summary: Manages a mapping of platform names to their respective classes and handles instantiation.
nodes.languageNotes: Provides methods for registration and creation of platforms.

nodes.name: session_manager.py
nodes.summary: Handles persistence and management of browser session cookies for different platforms.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses JSON for cookie storage on disk.

nodes.name: SessionManager
nodes.summary: Saves, loads, and clears browser cookies to maintain persistent sessions.
nodes.languageNotes: Asynchronous methods for disk I/O.

nodes.name: db_resilience.py
nodes.summary: Provides database resilience utilities like error mapping and retry logic with exponential backoff.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses 'tenacity' for retry logic and 'wraps' for decorators.

nodes.name: handle_db_errors
nodes.summary: Decorator that maps SQLAlchemy exceptions to custom domain exceptions.
nodes.languageNotes: Async-compatible decorator.

nodes.name: with_retry
nodes.summary: Decorator factory for applying retry logic with exponential backoff to async database operations.
nodes.languageNotes: Configurable attempts and wait times.

nodes.name: documents/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Initialization file for the documents core module.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard Python package initializer.

nodes.name: docx_renderer.py
nodes.summary: Renders resumes and cover letters to DOCX format using python-docx, offloading blocking I/O to threads.
nodes.languageNotes: Offloads blocking python-docx calls to thread executor.

nodes.name: DOCXRenderer
nodes.summary: Class for generating ATS-friendly DOCX documents from structured data.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles font settings, headers, and section formatting.

nodes.name: generator.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the document generation process, including LLM-based tailoring and parallel rendering to multiple formats.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses asyncio.gather for parallel rendering to PDF and DOCX.

nodes.name: DocumentGenerator
nodes.summary: Main entry point for generating tailored resumes and cover letters.
nodes.languageNotes: Coordinates between LLMClient, PDFRenderer, and DOCXRenderer.

nodes.name: parser.py
nodes.summary: Parses PDF and DOCX resumes to extract raw text, sections, contact info, and skills.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses PyPDF2 and python-docx for extraction.

nodes.name: DocumentParser
nodes.summary: Async parser that extracts structured information from resume files.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements regex-based section and contact info extraction.

nodes.name: pdf_renderer.py
nodes.summary: Renders Jinja2 HTML templates to PDF format using WeasyPrint.
nodes.languageNotes: Offloads WeasyPrint rendering to thread executor.

nodes.name: PDFRenderer
nodes.summary: Class for converting HTML templates or strings to PDF files.
nodes.languageNotes: Integrates Jinja2 for template rendering.

nodes.summary: Initialization for document templates.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package initializer.

nodes.name: templates/styles/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Initialization for document styles.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package initializer.

nodes.name: exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Defines the custom exception hierarchy for the entire application.
nodes.languageNotes: Hierarchical structure based on AutoApplyError.

nodes.name: job_discovery/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Initialization for job discovery module.

nodes.name: ExaJobSearch
nodes.summary: Encapsulates logic for searching jobs and company websites via Exa API.
nodes.languageNotes: Lazy-loads the Exa client.

nodes.summary: Client for making LLM requests with automatic provider fallback and cost tracking.

nodes.summary: Initialization for LLM prompts module.

nodes.summary: Templates and selection logic for generating various styles of cover letters using an LLM.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines multiple prompt styles (technical, creative, executive, etc.).

nodes.summary: LLM prompt templates and data schemas for tailoring resumes to specific job descriptions.

nodes.summary: Renders a resume tailoring prompt with provided resume data and job description.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Main application component, defining the routing and core layout.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses React Router for navigation.

nodes.summary: Component for displaying a summary of a job application's status and details.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Material UI for layout.

nodes.languageNotes: Class component implementing componentDidCatch.

nodes.name: LoadingState.tsx
nodes.summary: A reusable loading spinner component.
nodes.languageNotes: Stateless functional component.

nodes.name: ApplicationFunnel
nodes.summary: A React component that visualizes the job application funnel using a bar chart. It displays the progression of applications through various stages such as Pending, Applied, and Interview, utilizing Recharts for visualization and Material UI for the container layout.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles loading states and provides default empty states for the funnel stages.

nodes.name: StatsCards
nodes.summary: A dashboard component that renders a responsive grid of metric cards. It displays key search statistics including total jobs found, application count, interview invitations, and the average ATS match score, with specific iconography and color-coding for each metric.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a configuration-driven approach (STAT_ITEMS) for rendering metric cards consistently.

nodes.name: JobCard
nodes.summary: A reusable UI component for presenting job listing summaries. It displays the job title, company name, location, and match percentage score, along with metadata chips for platform, remote status, and salary range. It provides action buttons for viewing job details or initiating an application.
nodes.languageNotes: Features conditional rendering for the match score badge with color-coded success levels.

nodes.name: JobDetail.tsx
nodes.summary: A React component that displays detailed information about a job in a side drawer, including title, company, location, and match score.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Material UI Drawer and various icons for layout.

nodes.name: JobDetail
nodes.summary: The main functional component for JobDetail, fetching data via useJob hook and rendering the drawer content.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles loading and error states for job data.

nodes.name: JobFilters.tsx
nodes.summary: React component providing search and filter controls for the job listing page.
nodes.languageNotes: Integrates with Zustand useJobStore for state management.

nodes.name: JobFilters
nodes.summary: Manages the UI state for job filters, including search query, platform selection, and location.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Material UI Select, TextField, and Chip components.

nodes.name: AppLayout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root layout component that sets up the main application shell, including Header, Sidebar, and WebSocket connection.
nodes.languageNotes: Initializes global WebSocket connection on mount.

nodes.name: AppLayout
nodes.summary: Functional component for the app layout, managing the main content area and persistent UI elements.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses useWebSocket hook for real-time updates.

nodes.name: Header.tsx
nodes.summary: Top navigation bar component for the application.
nodes.languageNotes: Displays the application logo and sidebar toggle.

nodes.name: Header
nodes.summary: Functional component for the Header, providing the AppBar and Toolbar.
nodes.languageNotes: Accesses useAppStore to toggle sidebar visibility.

nodes.name: Sidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Side navigation drawer component containing links to different application sections.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports both desktop and mobile drawer variants.

nodes.name: Sidebar
nodes.summary: Functional component for the Sidebar, rendering navigation items based on the current route.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses react-router-dom useLocation and useNavigate.

nodes.name: ATSScoreCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Displays an ATS compatibility score along with skill gaps and improvement suggestions.
nodes.languageNotes: Visualizes scores using progress bars and chips.

nodes.name: ATSScoreCard
nodes.summary: Main component for rendering the ATS score summary and detailed feedback.
nodes.languageNotes: Parses the analysis result object to show missing skills.

nodes.name: ResumeUpload.tsx
nodes.summary: Component for uploading resume files with drag-and-drop support.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles file selection, drag events, and upload progress.

nodes.name: ResumeUpload
nodes.summary: Functional component managing the resume upload workflow.
nodes.languageNotes: Integrates with useUploadResume hook for API calls.

nodes.name: TemplateSelector.tsx
nodes.summary: UI component for selecting from various resume templates.
nodes.languageNotes: Renders a grid of template options with previews.

nodes.name: TemplateSelector
nodes.summary: Functional component for template selection, allowing users to choose a visual style.
nodes.languageNotes: Passes selected template ID back to parent via onSelect callback.

nodes.name: CandidateProfileEditor.tsx
nodes.summary: A comprehensive form for editing the candidate's personal profile, education, and experience.
nodes.languageNotes: Aggregates several sub-forms (EducationForm, ExperienceForm, SkillsEditor).

nodes.name: CandidateProfileEditor
nodes.summary: Main functional component for profile editing, managing complex nested form state.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses local state to track form changes before saving.

nodes.name: EducationForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Dynamic form for managing multiple education entries in the candidate profile.
nodes.languageNotes: Allows adding, editing, and removing education records.

nodes.name: EducationForm
nodes.summary: Functional component for education management, handling array-based state updates.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses MUI Card and TextField for each entry.

nodes.name: ExperienceForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Dynamic form for managing multiple work experience entries in the candidate profile.
nodes.languageNotes: Allows adding, editing, and removing work experience records.

nodes.name: ExperienceForm
nodes.summary: Functional component for experience management, handling array-based state updates.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses MUI Card and TextField for each entry.

nodes.name: LLMProvidersCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Displays a list of available and configured LLM providers for the application.
nodes.languageNotes: Shows provider status and configuration details.

nodes.name: LLMProvidersCard
nodes.summary: Functional component for rendering LLM provider information.
nodes.languageNotes: Displays provider names and active status.

nodes.name: SkillsEditor.tsx
nodes.summary: A tag-based editor for adding and removing skills from a list.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Material UI Chips to represent skills.

nodes.name: SkillsEditor
nodes.summary: Functional component for skill management, handling addition via input and removal via chip deletion.
nodes.languageNotes: Prevents duplicate skills and handles empty input.

nodes.name: useAnalytics.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hooks for fetching and managing application analytics data.
nodes.languageNotes: Exposes hooks for dashboard stats, funnel, and LLM usage.

nodes.name: useDashboardStats
nodes.summary: Hook to fetch high-level dashboard metrics.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses analyticsService.getDashboardStats.

nodes.name: useApplicationFunnel
nodes.summary: Hook to fetch application funnel data.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses analyticsService.getApplicationFunnel.

nodes.name: useLLMUsage
nodes.summary: Hook to fetch LLM token usage and costs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses analyticsService.getLLMUsage.

nodes.name: useApplications.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hooks for application-related CRUD operations and state transitions.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes hooks for listing, creating, and approving applications.

nodes.name: useApplications
nodes.summary: Hook to list job applications with pagination and filtering.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses appService.listApplications.

nodes.name: useCreateApplication
nodes.summary: Mutation hook for creating a new job application.
nodes.languageNotes: Invalidates applications query on success.

nodes.name: useJobs.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hooks for job-related operations, including listing, searching, and analyzing.
nodes.languageNotes: Integrates with jobService for API interactions.

nodes.name: useJobs
nodes.summary: Hook to fetch a paginated list of jobs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses jobService.listJobs.

nodes.name: useJob
nodes.summary: Hook to fetch details for a specific job.
nodes.languageNotes: Enabled only if jobId is provided.

nodes.name: useResumes.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hooks for resume management, uploading, and optimization.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles resume generation and ATS scoring.

nodes.name: useResumes
nodes.summary: Hook to fetch the list of candidate resumes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses resumeService.listResumes.

nodes.name: useUploadResume
nodes.summary: Mutation hook for uploading a resume file.
nodes.languageNotes: Invalidates resumes query on success.

nodes.name: useSettings.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hooks for fetching and updating user settings and LLM providers.
nodes.languageNotes: Integrates with settingsService.

nodes.name: useSettings
nodes.summary: Hook to fetch current user settings.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses settingsService.getSettings.

nodes.name: useWebSocket.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hook for managing persistent WebSocket connections with auto-reconnect logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements exponential backoff for reconnections.

nodes.name: useWebSocket
nodes.summary: Main functional hook for WebSocket management, handling events and connection state.
nodes.languageNotes: Returns connection status, last message, and a send function.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Application entry point that initializes React, QueryClient, and MUI theme provider.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ReactDOM.createRoot for concurrent mode rendering.

nodes.name: AnalyticsPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Page component displaying job search analytics using Recharts for visual data representation.
nodes.languageNotes: Integrates multiple data streams including LLM usage and ATS score distribution.

nodes.name: ApplicationsPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Page for tracking and managing job applications with status-based filtering and pagination.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses MUI Tabs for stage navigation.

nodes.name: DashboardPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Central overview page showing key statistics and recent application activity.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines stats cards and funnel charts for a quick pipeline overview.

nodes.name: JobSearchPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Job search interface allowing users to find listings across multiple platforms and apply.
nodes.languageNotes: Manages complex state for filters, search results, and job details.

nodes.name: ResumesPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Resume management page for uploading, template selection, and ATS score tracking.
nodes.languageNotes: Provides download links for generated PDF and DOCX formats.

nodes.name: SettingsPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Comprehensive settings page for configuring automation preferences, LLM providers, and candidate profile.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements real-time updates for sliders and switches via Mutation hooks.

nodes.name: analyticsService.ts
nodes.summary: API service for fetching dashboard statistics, funnel data, and usage metrics.
nodes.languageNotes: Exposes async functions mapping to backend analytics endpoints.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Centralized Axios configuration with trace ID injection and error normalization.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes interceptors for request tracing and consistent error response handling.

nodes.name: applicationService.ts
nodes.summary: Service for CRUD operations and status updates on job applications.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles both single and batch creation of applications.

nodes.name: jobService.ts
nodes.summary: Service for job searching, listing, and match analysis.
nodes.languageNotes: Interacts with multi-platform search and job analysis endpoints.

nodes.name: resumeService.ts
nodes.summary: Service for resume uploads, tailoring, scoring, and optimization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses multipart/form-data for file uploads.

nodes.summary: Service for managing user preferences and LLM provider status.
nodes.languageNotes: Fetches and updates global configuration settings.

nodes.summary: Vitest/Jest-dom test environment configuration.

nodes.summary: Global application state using Zustand for UI notifications, sidebar status, and connectivity.

layers.name: Backend API
layers.description: FastAPI routes, controllers, and API dependencies.

layers.name: Backend Core
layers.description: Core business logic, ATS analysis, automation engines, and system configuration.

layers.name: Backend Services
layers.description: Application services and background workers for asynchronous tasks.

layers.name: Backend Data
layers.description: Database models, schemas, migrations, and session management.

layers.name: Frontend UI
layers.description: React components, pages, styling, and static assets.

layers.name: Frontend Logic
layers.description: Custom hooks, state management, and API client services.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: DevOps configurations, build tools, and project-wide settings.

layers.name: Testing
layers.description: Unit, integration, and E2E tests for both backend and frontend.

layers.name: Templates
layers.description: Document templates for resumes and cover letters.

tour.title: Welcome to CareerPilot
tour.description: CareerPilot is an automated job application platform that uses AI to tailor resumes and apply to jobs. This tour will take you through the core components of the system, from the backend intelligence to the frontend user interface.

tour.title: Backend API & Routing
tour.description: The backend is built with FastAPI. It handles job searches, resume management, and application tracking. Requests flow through a central router to specialized endpoints for analytics, jobs, and applications.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI leverages Python's type hints for automatic request validation and documentation (Swagger/OpenAPI), making it highly developer-friendly for building robust APIs.

tour.title: Core Intelligence: LLM & ATS
tour.description: This layer represents the platform's 'brain'. It integrates with Large Language Models to analyze job descriptions and resumes, identifying skill gaps and tailoring content to maximize ATS (Applicant Tracking System) scores.
tour.languageLesson: The core logic uses advanced Python patterns like async/await for non-blocking LLM calls and structured Pydantic models for parsing complex AI responses.

tour.title: Job Automation Engine
tour.description: The automation engine executes the actual application process. It uses browser automation to interact with platforms like LinkedIn and Indeed, filling out forms and submitting applications on the user's behalf.

tour.title: Asynchronous Processing
tour.description: Long-running tasks like document generation and job applications are processed asynchronously. CareerPilot uses background workers and a queue system (Redis) to ensure the main API remains responsive.

tour.title: Frontend: React & State Management
tour.description: The frontend is a modern React application that provides a seamless user experience. It uses centralized stores for managing application state and hooks for interacting with backend services.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript is used throughout the frontend to provide strong typing, reducing runtime errors and improving the developer experience when managing complex data structures.

tour.title: User Interface: Dashboard & Analytics
tour.description: The final stop is the user interface where candidates manage their job search. The dashboard and analytics pages provide insights into application success rates and resume performance.
